REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF BECAUSE THERE IS NO RIPE ISSUE AND THERE IS NO NEED OF DETERMINATION OF FUTURE RIGHTS. (SEE DEL E. WEBB CORP. V. STRUCTURAL MATERIALS CO. (1981) 123 CAL.APP.3D 593, 604; BABB V. SUPERIOR COURT (1971) 3 CAL.3D 841, 848.)THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FACTS PLEADED BY P ARE SUFFICIENT TO STATE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EJECTMENT. (BAUGH V. CONSUMERS ASSOCIATES, LIMITED (1966) 241 CAL.APP.2D 672, 675.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD QUIDACHAY, CLERK K.LEE, NOT ON THE RECORD, D501

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Line 7
Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk Kevin Lee, Not On The Record, D501
Appearances:
Attorney Andrew Catterall, Esq. of Zaks Freedman (415) 596-8110, counsel for plaintiff / Opposing Party
Attorney Matt McFarland, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic (415) 771-9850, counsel for defendant / Moving Party
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard arguments from both sides rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Cherk's Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief because no actual controversy exists and there is no need of determination of future rights. (See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604; Babb v. Superior Court (1971) 3 Cal.3d 841, 848.) Order signed in open Court

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Line 7
Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk Kevin Lee, Not On The Record, D501
Appearances:
Attorney Andrew Catterall, Esq. of Zaks Freedman (415) 596-8110, counsel for plaintiff / Opposing Party
Attorney Matt McFarland, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic (415) 771-9850, counsel for defendant / Moving Party
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard arguments from both sides rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Pui's Demurrer is SUSTAINED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND and OVERRULED IN PART. The demurrer is sustained as to 1st Cause of Action. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief because there is no ripe issue and there is no need of determination of future rights. (See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604; Babb v. Superior Court (1971) 3 Cal.3d 841, 848.)The Demurrer is overruled as to 2nd Cause of Action. The facts pleaded by P are sufficient to state cause of action for ejectment. (Baugh v. Consumers Associates, Limited (1966) 241 Cal.App.2d 672, 675.) Order signed in open Court
